compile.scout: Inspect the source inventory and representative source excerpts. Update the durable source snapshot and proposed page plan, ask only high-cost owner decisions through the structured decision-question artifact, then stop. Do not write candidate pages yet.
compile.generate: Start the initial full compilation now. The owner has authorized execution without another plan-confirmation stop. Read the structured compile brief, cover the complete source set with provenance, handle uncertainty through best-guess plus tickets, and finish through candidate/index.md.
compile.regenerate: Regenerate the complete knowledge-base draft in this staging workspace. The owner has authorized execution without another plan-confirmation stop. Read the structured compile brief, cover the complete source set with provenance, handle uncertainty through best-guess plus tickets, and finish through candidate/index.md.
compile.approve_plan: The owner approved the exact current proposed plan. Execute it now, preserve the structured compile brief, handle uncertainty through best-guess plus tickets, and finish through candidate/index.md.
compile.incremental: Apply the structured source changeset as a scoped incremental compilation.
compile.resume: Resume the interrupted compiler-owned batch plan from its durable workspace state. Do not restart completed batches or ask for another approval.
compile.submit_decisions_header: Apply the following owner decisions to the durable brief and intent, then propose the resulting compile plan and stop for approval:
compile.apply_rulings_header: Apply the following structured rulings. Edit only the named candidate pages, remove the resolved uncertainty marker, and call resolve_ticket once per ruling with the exact dispatch nonce. After the repairs, resume any interrupted task:
compile.apply_rulings_suspect: - ticket {ticket_id} (pages: {pages}): keep both sources as accepted uncertainty; call resolve_ticket without changing the value; nonce: {nonce}
